contentBlocks.original:
नमो भूतनाथं नमो देवदेवं
नमः कालकालं नमो दिव्यतेजम् ।
नमः कामभस्मं नमः शान्तशीलं
भजे पार्वतीवल्लभं नीलकण्ठम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō bhūtanāthaṃ namō dēvadēvaṃ
namaḥ kālakālaṃ namō divyatējam ।
namaḥ kāmabhasmaṃ namaḥ śāntaśīlaṃ
bhajē pārvatīvallabhaṃ nīlakaṇṭham ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the lord of all beings — salutations to the God of gods — salutations to the death of Kāla (Kālakāla, conqueror of Death) — salutations to the divine radiance. Salutations to the one who reduced Kāma to ash — salutations to the tranquil-natured — I worship Śiva, the beloved of Pārvatī (Pārvatī-vallabha).
contentBlocks.meanings: bhūta-nātha: lord of all beings | deva-deva: God of gods | kāla-kāla: death of Kāla (Mṛtyuñjaya) | divya-tejas: divine radiance | kāma-bhasma: one who reduced Kāma to ash | śānta-śīla: tranquil-natured | pārvatī-vallabha: beloved of Pārvatī

contentBlocks.original:
सदा तीर्थसिद्धं सदा भक्तरक्षं
सदा शैवपूज्यं सदा शुभ्रभस्मम् ।
सदा ध्यानयुक्तं सदा ज्ञानतल्पं
भजे पार्वतीवल्लभं नीलकण्ठम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sadā tīrthasiddhaṃ sadā bhaktarakṣaṃ
sadā śaivapūjyaṃ sadā śubhrabhasmam ।
sadā dhyānayuktaṃ sadā jñānatalpaṃ
bhajē pārvatīvallabhaṃ nīlakaṇṭham ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who is eternally perfected in all holy places — who eternally protects his devotees — who is eternally worthy of worship by the Śaiva tradition — who is eternally pure with sacred ash (bhasma) — who is eternally absorbed in meditation — who is eternally the wisdom-Self — I worship Śiva, the beloved of Pārvatī.
contentBlocks.meanings: sadā tīrtha-siddha: eternally perfected in holy places | sadā bhakta-rakṣa: eternally protecting devotees | sadā śaiva-pūjya: eternally worshipped by Śaivas | sadā śubhra-bhasma: eternally with pure white ash | sadā dhyāna-yukta: eternally absorbed in meditation | sadā jñāna-ātman: eternally the wisdom-Self

contentBlocks.original:
श्मशाने शयानं महास्थानवासं
शरीरं गजानां सदा चर्मवेष्टम् ।
पिशाचादिनाथं पशूनां प्रतिष्ठं
भजे पार्वतीवल्लभं नीलकण्ठम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śmaśānē śayānaṃ mahāsthānavāsaṃ
śarīraṃ gajānāṃ sadā charmavēṣṭam ।
piśāchādināthaṃ paśūnāṃ pratiṣṭhaṃ
bhajē pārvatīvallabhaṃ nīlakaṇṭham ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who lies in the cremation ground — who dwells in great sacred places — whose body is always clothed in elephant skin — who is the lord of ghosts and goblins (piśācas) — who is the guardian of all creatures — I worship Śiva, the beloved of Pārvatī.
contentBlocks.meanings: śmaśāne śayāna: lying in the cremation ground | mahā-sthāna-vāsa: dwelling in great sacred places | śarīraṃ gajānāṃ carma-veṣṭa: body clothed in elephant skin | piśāca-ādi-nātha: lord of ghosts and goblins | paśūnāṃ pratiṣṭhā: guardian/support of all creatures

contentBlocks.original:
फणीनागकण्ठे भुजङ्गाद्यनेकं
गले रुण्डमालं महावीर शूरम् ।
कटिव्याघ्रचर्मं चिताभस्मलेपं
भजे पार्वतीवल्लभं नीलकण्ठम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phaṇīnāgakaṇṭhē bhujaṅgādyanēkaṃ
gaḻē ruṇḍamālaṃ mahāvīra śūram ।
kaṭivyāghracharmaṃ chitābhasmalēpaṃ
bhajē pārvatīvallabhaṃ nīlakaṇṭham ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who wears at his throat the great serpent (Nāga) along with many serpents — who bears at his neck the garland of skulls — the great hero, the brave one — whose waist is wrapped in tiger-skin — smeared with cremation ash — I worship Śiva, the beloved of Pārvatī.
contentBlocks.meanings: phaṇī-nāga-kaṇṭha: serpent (Nāga) at the throat | bhujanga-aneka: many serpents | gale ruṇḍa-mālā: garland of skulls at the neck | mahā-vīra śūra: great hero, brave one | kaṭi-vyāghra-carma: tiger-skin at the waist | citā-bhasma-lepa: smeared with cremation ash

contentBlocks.original:
शिरः शुद्धगङ्गा शिवा वामभागं
वियद्दीर्घकेशं सदा मां त्रिणेत्रम् ।
फणीनागकर्णं सदा फालचन्द्रं
भजे पार्वतीवल्लभं नीलकण्ठम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śiraḥ śuddhagaṅgā śivā vāmabhāgaṃ
viyaddīrghakēśaṃ sadā māṃ triṇētram ।
phaṇīnāgakarṇaṃ sadā phālachandraṃ
bhajē pārvatīvallabhaṃ nīlakaṇṭham ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: On his head, the pure Gaṅgā — at his left side, Śivā (Pārvatī) — with sky-touching long hair — with three eyes always — with serpents as earrings, with serpents ever fanning him — I worship Śiva, the beloved of Pārvatī.
contentBlocks.meanings: śiraḥ: on the head | śuddha-gaṅgā: pure Gaṅgā | śivā: Śivā (Pārvatī, the auspicious goddess) | vāma-bhāga: left side | viyat-dīrgha-keśa: sky-touching long hair | tri-netra: three-eyed | phaṇī-nāga-karṇa: serpents as earrings | phaṇī-nāga: serpents fanning

contentBlocks.original:
करे शूलधारं महाकष्टनाशं
सुरेशं परेशं महेशं जनेशम् ।
धनेशामरेशं ध्वजेशं गिरीशं
भजे पार्वतीवल्लभं नीलकण्ठम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karē śūladhāraṃ mahākaṣṭanāśaṃ
surēśaṃ parēśaṃ mahēśaṃ janēśam ।
dhanēśāmarēśaṃ dhvajēśaṃ girīśaṃ
bhajē pārvatīvallabhaṃ nīlakaṇṭham ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Trident-bearer in hand — destroyer of great afflictions — lord of gods (Sureśa), supreme lord (Pareśa), great lord (Maheśa), lord of all people (Janeśa) — lord of Kubera (wealth), lord of the immortals (Amareśa), lord of banners (Dhvajeśa), lord of mountains (Girīśa) — I worship Śiva, the beloved of Pārvatī.
contentBlocks.meanings: śūla-dhāra: trident-bearer | mahā-kaṣṭa-nāśa: destroyer of great afflictions | sureśa: lord of gods | pareśa: supreme lord | maheśa: great lord | janeśa: lord of all people | dhaneśa: lord of Kubera/wealth | amareśa: lord of immortals | dhvajeśa: lord of banners | girīśa: lord of mountains

contentBlocks.original:
उदासं सुदासं सुकैलासवासं
धरानिर्झरे संस्थितं ह्यादिदेवम् ।
अजं हेमकल्पद्रुमं कल्पसेव्यं
भजे पार्वतीवल्लभं नीलकण्ठम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
udāsaṃ sudāsaṃ sukailāsavāsaṃ
dharānirjharē saṃsthitaṃ hyādidēvam ।
ajaṃ hēmakalpadrumaṃ kalpasēvyaṃ
bhajē pārvatīvallabhaṃ nīlakaṇṭham ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The detached one, the supremely devoted servant — the divine abode-dweller on Mount Kailāsa — who is established in the running streams of earth — the primordial God — the wish-fulfilling golden divine tree (kalpa-druma) — the eternal abode of liberation — I worship Śiva, the beloved of Pārvatī.
contentBlocks.meanings: udāsa: detached one | su-dāsa: supremely devoted servant | su-kailāsa-vāsa: beautiful Kailāsa-dweller | dharā-nirzara: in the running streams of earth | ādi-deva: primordial God | hema-kalpa-druma: golden wish-fulfilling tree | kalpa-sevā: service of liberation | nitya-dhāma: eternal abode

contentBlocks.original:
मुनीनां वरेण्यं गुणं रूपवर्णं
द्विजैः सम्पठन्तं शिवं वेदशास्त्रम् ।
अहो दीनवत्सं कृपालुं शिवं तं
भजे पार्वतीवल्लभं नीलकण्ठम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
munīnāṃ varēṇyaṃ guṇaṃ rūpavarṇaṃ
dvijaiḥ sampaṭhantaṃ śivaṃ vēdaśāstram ।
ahō dīnavatsaṃ kṛpāluṃ śivaṃ taṃ
bhajē pārvatīvallabhaṃ nīlakaṇṭham ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who is the foremost of sages in quality and beauty — who is chanted by the twice-born with Vedas and sacred texts (śāstras) — alas, who is tender-hearted toward the destitute — full of grace — I worship Śiva, the auspicious one (Śiva), the beloved of Pārvatī.
contentBlocks.meanings: munīnāṃ vareṇya: foremost among sages | guṇa-rūpa-varṇa: in quality, form, and complexion | dvijaiḥ sampaṭhan: chanted by the twice-born | śivaṃ veda-śāstram: the auspicious Veda-scriptures | aho dīna-vatsa: O tender-hearted toward the destitute | kṛpā-guṇa: quality of grace

contentBlocks.original:
सदा भावनाथं सदा सेव्यमानं
सदा भक्तिदेवं सदा पूज्यमानम् ।
महातीर्थवासं सदा सेव्यमेकं
भजे पार्वतीवल्लभं नीलकण्ठम् ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sadā bhāvanāthaṃ sadā sēvyamānaṃ
sadā bhaktidēvaṃ sadā pūjyamānam ।
mahātīrthavāsaṃ sadā sēvyamēkaṃ
bhajē pārvatīvallabhaṃ nīlakaṇṭham ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who is eternally the lord of all feelings and devotion — who is eternally served and worshipped — who is eternally the God of devotion — who is eternally revered — who dwells in the great holy places — who alone is eternally served — I worship Śiva, the beloved of Pārvatī, the adorned one. May this aṣṭakam bring all blessings.
contentBlocks.meanings: sadā bhāva-nātha: eternally lord of all devotional feelings | sadā sevyamāna: eternally served and worshipped | sadā bhakti-deva: eternally the God of devotion | sadā pūjyamāna: eternally revered | mahā-tīrtha-vāsa: dwelling in great holy places | sadā sevya-eka: the one alone eternally to be served | pārvatī-vallabha: beloved of Pārvatī
